channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Banksy confirms new statue installed in central London is his work
item.description: The statue in Waterloo Place, which appeared early Wednesday, depicts a man proudly hoisting a flag— but the flag is blinding him.
item.content:encoded:
The statue in Waterloo Place, which appeared early Wednesday, depicts a man proudly hoisting a flag— but the flag is blinding him.

(Image credit: Kin Cheung)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Imprisoned Iranian Nobel Peace Prize winner in critical condition
item.description: Narges Mohammadi, an Iranian activist and 2023 Nobel Peace Prize laureate, was hospitalized after collapsing in prison. Her family says her condition has deteriorated since a March heart attack.
item.content:encoded:
Narges Mohammadi, an Iranian activist and 2023 Nobel Peace Prize laureate, was hospitalized after collapsing in prison. Her family says her condition has deteriorated since a March heart attack.

(Image credit: Rune Hellestad)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Opinion: The everyday tragedy of gun violence
item.description: The White House Correspondents Association Dinner was one of several incidents of gun violence in the U.S. last week. Others ended in injuries and fatalities.
item.content:encoded:
The White House Correspondents Association Dinner was one of several incidents of gun violence in the U.S. last week. Others ended in injuries and fatalities.

(Image credit: ROBERTO SCHMIDT/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: Can Trump's latest pick for surgeon general make it through confirmation?
item.description: Nicole Saphier, a breast cancer radiologist, is the president's third nominee for surgeon general. Will she get confirmed?
item.content:encoded:
Nicole Saphier, a breast cancer radiologist, is the president's third nominee for surgeon general. Will she get confirmed?

(Image credit: Theo Wargo)
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: Americans aren't sleeping enough. Here's what could help
item.description: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.
item.content:encoded: Nearly a third of Americans get less than the recommended seven hours of sleep a night. A lot of us struggle to get to bed as we power through tasks or get lost in endless scrolling. Here's help.
item.dc:creator: Pien Huang

item.title: New Netflix documentary reexamines Winnie Mandela's divisive legacy
item.description: Winnie Madikizela-Mandela is one of the most revered — and controversial — women in South African history. In a new documentary her granddaughters examine the liberation icon in all her complexity.
item.content:encoded: Winnie Madikizela-Mandela is one of the most revered — and controversial — women in South African history. In a new documentary her granddaughters examine the liberation icon in all her complexity.
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: After Assad's fall, Syria's Kurds are left in limbo, feeling abandoned by the U.S.
item.description: Caught in limbo after the fall of Syria's Bashar al-Assad, Kurdish families struggle with cold, loss and uncertainty — feeling abandoned by the U.S. allies they once fought alongside.
item.content:encoded:
Caught in limbo after the fall of Syria's Bashar al-Assad, Kurdish families struggle with cold, loss and uncertainty — feeling abandoned by the U.S. allies they once fought alongside.

(Image credit: Claire Harbage)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: 2026 Senate races to watch: From most likely to flip to Democratic long shots
item.description: Democrats could have a narrow path to winning the Senate, with these specific states on the table.
item.content:encoded:
Democrats could have a narrow path to winning the Senate, with these specific states on the table.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro

item.title: Spirit Airlines ceases operations after escalating financial struggles
item.description: The low-cost carrier, which had been struggling for years, announced it will cease operations. Spirit had been seeking a $500 million lifeline from the White House, but talks failed to yield a deal.
item.content:encoded:
The low-cost carrier, which had been struggling for years, announced it will cease operations. Spirit had been seeking a $500 million lifeline from the White House, but talks failed to yield a deal.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Air Force says former Qatari 747 will be ready to fly as Air Force One this summer
item.description: The U.S. Air Force has finished modifying and testing a Boeing 747 jet donated by Qatar for temporary use as Air Force One and expects to have it ready for President Trump to use this summer.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. Air Force has finished modifying and testing a Boeing 747 jet donated by Qatar for temporary use as Air Force One and expects to have it ready for President Trump to use this summer.

(Image credit: Matt Rourke)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
